rule.name: jpcite-burn-monitor-schedule
rule.description: Fires scripts/aws_credit_ops/continuous_burn_monitor.sh once an hour for sustained credit burn auto-resubmit. DISABLED by default; enable explicitly after one dry-run validation tick. The monitor honors a $18,900 hard-stop sentinel and $16,065 slowdown line; the AWS Budget Action attached to jpcite-credit-run-stop-18900 is the structural backstop.

lambda_runner.name: jpcite-burn-monitor
lambda_runner.description: Lambda wrapper that invokes continuous_burn_monitor.sh logic (rewritten in python for Lambda runtime). The shell version under scripts/aws_credit_ops/continuous_burn_monitor.sh is the operator-driven canonical; the Lambda is the cadence executor.

iam_role.name: jpcite-burn-monitor-schedule-role
iam_role.description: Role assumed by events.amazonaws.com to invoke the burn-monitor Lambda. Lambda execution role separately grants sagemaker:CreateTransformJob, batch:SubmitJob, ce:GetCostAndUsage, sagemaker:ListTransformJobs, batch:ListJobs.

cadence_economics.notes: The monitor itself is cheap (a few API calls + sentinel write). Cost burn comes from the SageMaker / Batch submissions it triggers when quota slots free. Cooldown gates (SM=30min, GPU=60min) prevent stacking.

operator_notes.default_mode: DRY_RUN (the shell script defaults to dry-run; commit_mode in the EventBridge input flips it on)
